title: triticum spelta var. duhamelianum
intro: Triticum spelta var. duhamelianum, also known as spelt, is a type of wheat that belongs to the family Poaceae, characterized by its distinctive tetraploid genetic makeup and used for both food and medicinal purposes. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial effects, making it a potential treatment for various health issues. The health benefits of spelt include improved digestion, reduced inflammation, and enhanced immune function, as well as potential anti-cancer and cardiovascular benefits. Spelt can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties for therapeutic purposes.
